gloss.en: a man of high social rank or noble birth

words.example: The nobleman lived in a grand castle.

gloss.en: a person who moves from place to place rather than settling

words.example: The nomad traveled across the desert with his camels.

gloss.en: where smoking is not allowed

words.example: We asked for a non-smoking room at the hotel.

gloss.en: not done as a paid job; not expert

words.example: The play was performed by nonprofessional actors.

gloss.en: a usual or expected pattern of behaviour

words.example: The new design is a departure from the norm.

gloss.en: towards the north

words.example: The birds flew northward as winter ended.

gloss.en: towards the north

words.example: They drove northwards along the coast.

gloss.en: one of the two openings of the nose

words.example: The smoke made his nostril sting.

gloss.en: a small book of plain paper for writing notes

words.example: The police officer wrote the details down in his notebook.

gloss.en: an idea, belief or understanding of something

words.example: He had no notion of how hard the work would be.

gloss.en: the figure zero

words.example: The number begins with a nought.

gloss.en: to give food needed to grow and stay healthy

words.example: Good food helps to nourish the body.

gloss.en: the food needed to grow and stay healthy

words.example: Babies get nourishment from their mother's milk.

gloss.en: a person who writes novels

words.example: She is a famous historical novelist.

gloss.en: the quality of being new and unusual

words.example: The novelty of the toy soon wore off.

gloss.en: at the present time, unlike the past

words.example: Nowadays most kids prefer going online to reading books.

gloss.en: a person or thing that annoys or causes trouble

words.example: The broken lift is a real nuisance.

gloss.en: relating to or shown in numbers

words.example: Please list the items in numerical order.

gloss.en: existing in large numbers

words.example: He has been late on numerous occasions.

gloss.en: the job of caring for sick or injured people

words.example: She wants a career in nursing.

gloss.en: a small hard fruit with a hard shell

words.example: He used a tool to crack the nut.

gloss.en: the process of getting the food needed to be healthy

words.example: The doctor gave advice on diet and nutrition.

gloss.en: the hard outer shell of a nut

words.example: In a nutshell, the plan failed.

gloss.en: a formal promise to do something or tell the truth

words.example: The witness took an oath to tell the truth.

gloss.en: the act of doing what you are told to do

words.example: The dog was trained for obedience.

gloss.en: doing what you are told to do

words.example: She is a quiet and obedient child.

gloss.en: the state of being very fat in an unhealthy way

words.example: Obesity can increase the risk of heart disease.

gloss.en: to do what you are told or expected to do

words.example: Soldiers must obey orders.

gloss.en: a printed notice of a person's death and life

words.example: His obituary appeared in the newspaper.

gloss.en: a thing that can be seen and touched

words.example: There was a strange object on the table.

gloss.en: based on facts, not personal feelings

words.example: We need an objective assessment of the situation.

gloss.en: a duty you must do by law or because it is right

words.example: You are under no obligation to buy anything.

gloss.en: the act of watching somebody or something carefully

words.example: The data came from direct observation of the animals.

gloss.en: to see or notice somebody or something

words.example: Have you observed any changes lately?

gloss.en: a person who watches somebody or something

words.example: An observer saw the plane explode after take-off.

gloss.en: thinking about something too much in an extreme way

words.example: He has an obsessive interest in cleaning.

gloss.en: a thing that makes it hard to achieve something

words.example: We managed to overcome every obstacle in our path.

gloss.en: to get something, especially by making an effort

words.example: You can obtain more information online.

gloss.en: sometimes but not often

words.example: We occasionally meet for a drink after work.

gloss.en: a job or profession

words.example: Please state your name, age and occupation.

gloss.en: to fill or use space, an area or time

words.example: The bed seemed to occupy most of the room.

gloss.en: strange or unusual

words.example: They're very odd people.

gloss.en: an illegal act; a crime

words.example: Stealing is a serious offence.

gloss.en: to make somebody feel upset by being rude

words.example: They'll be offended if you don't come.

gloss.en: a person who commits a crime

words.example: The police arrested the young offender.

gloss.en: an illegal act; a crime

words.example: Drunk driving is a serious offense.

gloss.en: rude and causing upset or disrespect

words.example: He made some offensive remarks.

gloss.en: without time to think or prepare

words.example: I can't tell you the price offhand.

gloss.en: agreed or done by a person in authority

words.example: They made an official announcement today.

gloss.en: located in the sea, away from the coast

words.example: The company built an offshore wind farm.

gloss.en: a smooth cream put on the skin to heal it

words.example: She put some ointment on the cut.

gloss.en: a small green or black fruit used for food and oil

words.example: He added an olive to the salad.

gloss.en: an ancient Greek site of the original games

words.example: The ancient games were held at Olympia.

gloss.en: a celebration of the Olympic Games or a contest

words.example: She won a medal at the maths olympiad.

gloss.en: continuing to exist or develop

words.example: There is an ongoing debate about the plan.

gloss.en: a space or hole something can pass through

words.example: We could see the stars through an opening in the roof.

gloss.en: without hiding feelings, opinions or information

words.example: Can you talk openly with your parents?

gloss.en: a play set to music in which the words are sung

words.example: We went to see an Italian opera.

gloss.en: to work in a particular way

words.example: Most freezers operate at very low temperatures.

gloss.en: a person who operates a machine or equipment

words.example: He works as a machine operator.

gloss.en: a person you play or fight against

words.example: She beat her opponent in the final.

gloss.en: to strongly disagree with and try to stop something

words.example: Many people oppose the new law.

gloss.en: strongly disagreeing with and trying to stop something

words.example: He was strongly opposed to the plan.

gloss.en: the act of strongly disagreeing to prevent something

words.example: The opposition came mainly from students.

gloss.en: a person who expects good things to happen

words.example: An optimist always sees the bright side.

gloss.en: expecting good things or success to happen

words.example: We are now taking a more optimistic view.

gloss.en: that you can choose to do or not do

words.example: The evening trip is optional.

gloss.en: a person who gives powerful public speeches

words.example: He was a brilliant orator who moved the crowd.

gloss.en: a large group of people playing instruments together

words.example: She plays the flute in the school orchestra.

gloss.en: relating to or for an orchestra

words.example: We listened to an orchestral piece by Mozart.

gloss.en: a plant with bright, unusual flowers

words.example: She grows a rare orchid in her garden.

gloss.en: a body part with a special purpose, like the heart

words.example: The heart is a vital organ.

gloss.en: produced without artificial chemicals

words.example: We only buy organic vegetables.

gloss.en: a person who arranges an event

words.example: She is the organiser of the festival.

gloss.en: a person who makes arrangements for something

words.example: The organizers of the festival worked hard.

gloss.en: the point where something starts; its cause

words.example: Scientists study the origin of life on earth.

gloss.en: the quality of being new and not a copy

words.example: Her work shows great originality.

gloss.en: at the beginning, before something changed

words.example: The school was originally very small.

gloss.en: an object used to make a place look nicer

words.example: She placed a small ornament on the shelf.

gloss.en: used to say what would happen if things were different

words.example: Take a map, otherwise you might get lost.

gloss.en: a unit of weight equal to about 28 grams

words.example: Add an ounce of butter to the bowl.

gloss.en: the remote, dry inland areas of Australia

words.example: They drove deep into the Australian outback.

gloss.en: the result or effect of an action or event

words.example: The likely outcome is a compromise.

gloss.en: to go faster and leave others far behind

words.example: The runner began to outdistance her rivals.

gloss.en: to do better than someone else

words.example: He always tries to outdo his brother.

gloss.en: on the outside of something

words.example: The outer layers of the skin are dry.

gloss.en: a set of clothes worn together

words.example: She was wearing an expensive new outfit.

gloss.en: to grow too big or too old for something

words.example: Children quickly outgrow their shoes.

gloss.en: a description of the main facts or points

words.example: This is a brief outline of the events.

gloss.en: to live or last longer than someone or something

words.example: She outlived her husband by many years.

gloss.en: a patient treated without staying in hospital

words.example: He was treated as an outpatient at the clinic.

gloss.en: the amount that a person or machine produces

words.example: Manufacturing output has increased this year.

gloss.en: shocking and unacceptable

words.example: The prices in that shop are outrageous.

gloss.en: to be much better than someone or something

words.example: She managed to outshine all the other singers.

gloss.en: of or on the outer side

words.example: The outside walls are damp.

gloss.en: the outer parts of a town or city

words.example: They live on the outskirts of the city.

gloss.en: extremely good; excellent

words.example: He is an outstanding player.

gloss.en: on the outside; in appearance

words.example: She seemed outwardly calm before the exam.

gloss.en: shaped like an egg or a long circle

words.example: The table has an oval shape.

gloss.en: including everything; general

words.example: There will be one overall winner.

gloss.en: over the side of a boat into the water

words.example: A sailor fell overboard during the storm.

gloss.en: to sell more seats or rooms than are available

words.example: Airlines sometimes overbook their flights.

gloss.en: a long warm coat worn over other clothes

words.example: He put on his overcoat before going out.

gloss.en: to succeed in dealing with a problem

words.example: She overcame injury to win the gold medal.

gloss.en: to give too much importance to something

words.example: Don't overemphasise the small mistakes.

gloss.en: to give too much importance to something

words.example: We should not overemphasize the risks.

gloss.en: to think something is bigger or better than it is

words.example: Don't overestimate how much time you have.

gloss.en: to hear what others say without them knowing

words.example: I happened to overhear their conversation.

gloss.en: a shared part where two things cover each other

words.example: There is some overlap between the two jobs.

gloss.en: during or for the night

words.example: We took an overnight train to London.

gloss.en: connected with foreign countries across the sea

words.example: The firm is expanding into overseas markets.

gloss.en: to make something seem less important
gloss.zh: 使黯然失色；给…蒙上阴影

words.example: His success was overshadowed by his brother's.

gloss.en: larger than the usual size

words.example: She wore an oversize sweater.

gloss.en: to go beyond what is allowed or proper

words.example: He overstepped his authority at work.

gloss.en: to pass someone or something moving ahead

words.example: The car tried to overtake the truck.

gloss.en: to remove a leader or government by force

words.example: The rebels tried to overthrow the government.

gloss.en: extra time spent working beyond normal hours

words.example: He worked overtime to finish the project.

gloss.en: a sound made to show sudden pain

words.example: Ow! That really hurt my finger.

gloss.en: to have to pay or repay money to somebody

words.example: She still owes her father some money.

gloss.en: a bird that hunts at night with large eyes

words.example: An owl hooted in the dark forest.
